macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Previous Discussion on Shomer Liability

micro_segments.title: Opening: Summary of Mishnah on Excluded Categories from Ona'ah, Shvuah, and Shomrim
Shomer Chinam and Shomer Sachar Exemptions from Oath and Payment
Unaddressed Case: Negligent Shomer Who Admits to Negligence

macro_segments.title: Shekalim Case: Ownership and Liability When Money Is Stolen or Lost

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: People of Town Send Shekalim to Beis HaMikdash; Theft or Loss Scenarios
Explanation of Terumat HaLishka and Conceptual Ownership Transfer
Distinction: Before vs. After Teruma Taken; Who Bears Liability
Found or Recovered Money: Eilu V'Eilu Shekalim—Both Sanctified
Question on Shvuah by Hekdesh: Why Swear on Sanctified Objects?

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Answer: Messengers Taking Oath to Collect Their Fee

micro_segments.title: Shmuel: The Messengers Are Not Swearing to Exempt Themselves, but to Collect Payment
Gemara Challenge: Why Swear to Gizbarin if the Villagers Pay the Messengers?
Rabbah's Answer: Oath to Villagers in Presence of Gizbarin to Clear Suspicion

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Objection: Messengers' Negligence Liability Should Bar Fee Collection

micro_segments.title: Gemara: Even if Exempt from Payment Due to Hekdesh, Negligence Should Forfeit Fee
Distinction: Shomer Sachar Liability and Duty of Care vs. Payment Obligation
Gemara Position: Oath Cannot Salvage Fee Collection If Negligent

macro_segments.title: Rava's Resolution: Armed Robbery and Shipwreck as Force Majeure

micro_segments.title: Rava: Cases Involve Unavoidable Disasters (Armed Bandits, Sinking Ship), Not Negligence
Logic: No Shirking of Duty of Care When Loss is Oneis (Force Majeure)

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Alternative: Shekels Are Hekdesh for Which Messengers Bear Responsibility

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon's Principle: Hekdesh You Are Liable For Can Have Shvuah
Gemara Analysis: Principle Works Before Teruma, But Not After
Braita: Teruma Works on Lost, Collected, and Future Money (Tormin Al Ha-Avud)
Explanation of L'Asid Ligvos: Conceptual Inclusion of Future Contributions

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar's Takana: Swearing on Hekdesh to Prevent Negligence

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar: Takana (Rabbinical Enactment) Requires Oath on Hekdesh for Responsibility
Policy Rationale: Without Oath Obligation, People Would Be Negligent Toward Hekdesh
Discussion: Scope of Takana—Limited to Shekalim or General Principle?

macro_segments.title: Shomer Sachar for Hekdesh: Parah Adumah, Children, Seeds, and Kinyan Sudar

micro_segments.title: Cases: Guarding Parah Adumah, Babysitters, Seeds; Paid on Weekdays But Not Shabbat
Havla'ah Concept: Weekly or Monthly Pay Includes Shabbat Work Allowably
Problem: How Can Shomer Be Liable to Hekdesh Without Monetary Nexus?
Rav Sheshet and Rabbi Yochanan: Kinyan Sudar—Special Contract to Accept Liability
Application: How Kinyan Sudar Closes Gaps in Hekdesh Exemptions

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon on Kodashim: Liable for Those You Bear Responsibility For

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon: Kodashim You Are Liable For—You Are Chayav; Those You Aren't—You Are Patur
Braita Analysis: Pasukim Show Chiyuv on Mixed Hekdesh-Private Objects
Word Analysis: Ba-Hashem V'Kichesh and Be-Amito—Distinguishing Hekdesh from Private
Principle: If Amito Element Exists (Shared Liability), You Are Chayav; If Not, Patur

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehudah on Ona'ah Exemptions: Sefer Torah, Jewels, Animals, War Materials

micro_segments.title: Sefer Torah: No Ona'ah Because Its Value Is Infinite (Lo Ein Ketz L'Dameha)
Jewels and Animals: No Ona'ah When Matching or Pairing (L'Zavgan)
Gemara Pushback: Many Items Have Matching Value; What Distinguishes These?
Rabbi Yehudah Distinction: These Are Particularly Valued; Others Are Not
Limit: Doubling the Price (Ad Kedei Dmeihem) Constitutes Overcharging
Rabbi Yehudah ben Beteira: War Goods (Horse, Sword, Shield) No Ona'ah—Chayei Nefesh
Rationale: Desperate Need Sets Different Market; No Overcharging Claim Possible

macro_segments.title: Ona'at Devarim: Afflicting with Words More Severe Than Financial Cheating

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Don't Ask Price of Merchandise with No Intent to Buy
Modern Application: Window Shopping and Online Research Strategy
Distinction: Business/Financial Component vs. Purely Emotional Harm
Mishnah Cases: Ba'al Teshuvah, Ben Gerim—Recalling Past Misdeeds
Torah Source: Veger Lo Toneh V'Lo Tilchatzenu (Exodus)—Focus on Emotional Injury
Braita: Ba'al Teshuvah, Ben Gerim, Ger Learned—Don't Shame Them
Suffering and Sickness: Don't Make Job-Like Accusations of Hidden Sins
Fool's Errand: Sending Someone to Non-Existent Merchant
Rabbi Yehudah: Even Without Words—Don't Inspect Goods Without Money
Davar Masur L'Lev: Hidden Intentions—Only God Knows; Fear of God Required

macro_segments.title: Comparative Severity: Ona'at Devarim vs. Ona'at Mammon

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan (Rabbi Shimon ben Yochai): Ona'at Devarim More Serious Than Money Cheating
Distinction: Yirat Elohim Warning in Devarim but Not in Mammon—Imminent Punishment
Rabbi Elazar: Words Injure the Person; Money Injures the Pocket
Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani: Money Can Be Repaid; Words Cannot Be Taken Back

topical_tags.term: Havla'ah (wage aggregation to permit Shabbat payment)
Rabbi Yehudah ben Beteira (Tanna on war goods)
Rabbi Elazar (Amora on severity of Ona'at Devarim)
Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani (Amora on irreversibility of words)
Davar Masur L'Lev (matter left to the heart/intention)
Yirat Elohim (fear of God as motivation)
